schools.summary: The making of the altar of burnt offering: 'and he made the altar of burnt offering of shittim wood: five cubits was the length thereof, and five cubits the breadth thereof; it was foursquare; and three cubits the height thereof' — the brazen altar of burnt offering made of acacia wood overlaid with brass (vv.2), five cubits square and three high, set in the court (the altar, the great altar of sacrifice at the entrance of the court, Exodus 27:1-8, the foursquare, the altar square, the figure of the four corners of the earth, the sacrifice for all, the place of sacrifice, where the burnt offerings were offered, the blood shed, the atonement made); the place of atonement — the altar of burnt offering, where the sacrifices were offered for the expiation of sin (the witnesses noting the figure — the altar a figure of the cross of Christ, the place of the great sacrifice, Christ 'offered himself,' Hebrews 9:14, the altar that sanctifies the gift, Matthew 23:19, the cross the true altar where the Lamb of God was offered, the brass, the altar overlaid with brass, enduring the fire, the figure of Christ enduring the fire of God's wrath, bearing the judgment for sin, the burnt offering, the whole offering, wholly consumed, the figure of Christ's entire self-offering, Ephesians 5:2, the first altar met at the entrance, no approach to God but by the altar of sacrifice, by the blood, Hebrews 9:22, the cross the way of access); the altar of burnt offering (the place of the sacrifices, the expiation) — the figure (the cross, the place of atonement, the providence, the altar made, the figure of the great sacrifice, no approach to God but by the altar) — Henry, Calvin, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the altar of burnt offering signify?

sides.position: The place of sacrifice and the expiation of sin (Exodus 27:1-8) - a figure of the cross of Christ where the Lamb of God was offered (Hebrews 9:14; Ephesians 5:2), the brass enduring the fire of God's wrath, no approach to God but by the altar of sacrifice (Hebrews 9:22)

themes: And he made the altar of burnt offering of shittim wood: five cubits was the length thereof, and five cubits the breadth thereof; it was foursquare; and three cubits the height thereof
the making of the altar of burnt offering (the crux of the place of sacrifice and the figure of the cross)
the place of sacrifice and the expiation of sin (Exodus 27:1-8)
a figure of the cross where Christ offered himself, the way of access to God (Hebrews 9:14; Ephesians 5:2)

schools.summary: The making of the vessels of the altar: 'and he made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, and the shovels, and the basons, and the fleshhooks, and the firepans: all the vessels thereof made he of brass' — all the vessels of the brazen altar (pots for ashes, shovels, basins for blood, fleshhooks, firepans) made of brass (the vessels, the instruments for the service of the altar, Exodus 27:3, the brass, all of brass, the metal of the altar and its furniture, the service, the vessels for handling the ashes, the blood, the flesh, and the fire); the pots, shovels, and basins (for the ashes and the blood, Exodus 27:3) — the service (the instruments of the sacrifice); 'the fleshhooks, and the firepans: all the vessels thereof made he of brass' (the fleshhooks, for the flesh, and firepans, for the coals, all of brass, the providence, the vessels of the altar, all of brass, the furniture of the sacrifice, the figure of the various means and instruments of the one sacrifice) — Ellicott.

themes: And he made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, and the shovels, and the basons, and the fleshhooks, and the firepans: all the vessels thereof made he of brass
the making of the vessels of the altar
the instruments for the service of the altar, all of brass (Exodus 27:3)
the furniture of the sacrifice

schools.summary: The rings for the staves of the altar: 'and he cast four rings for the four ends of the grate of brass, to be places for the staves' — four brass rings cast at the four ends of the grate, as holders for the staves (poles) to carry the altar (the rings, the holders for the staves, Exodus 27:4, the staves, the poles for bearing the altar, the altar portable, the bearing, the altar carried on the journey, like the ark, the place of sacrifice moving with the people); the four rings (for the staves, the altar made portable, Exodus 27:4-5) — the bearing (the altar carried, the sacrifice ever with the people on the way); 'to be places for the staves' (the rings for the staves, the altar borne by the poles, the figure, the altar of sacrifice moving with the pilgrim people, the cross ever before them, the providence, the altar made portable, the place of sacrifice with the people in the wilderness) — JFB.

themes: And he cast four rings for the four ends of the grate of brass, to be places for the staves
the rings for the staves of the altar
the altar made portable, borne by the poles (Exodus 27:4-5)
the place of sacrifice borne with the pilgrim people

schools.summary: The laver made of the women's mirrors: 'and he made the laver of brass, and the foot of it of brass, of the lookingglasses of the women assembling, which assembled at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation' — the making — 'he made the laver of brass... of the lookingglasses of the women assembling, which assembled at the door of the tabernacle': the brazen laver (the basin for the priests' washing, Exodus 30:18-21) made of the brass mirrors (looking-glasses, polished brass mirrors, not glass) freely given by the women who assembled (served, ministered, or gathered) at the door of the tabernacle (the witnesses noting the significance — the women's mirrors, the instruments of vanity and self-adornment, given up and made into the laver of cleansing, the figure — the self-denial of the women, renouncing their vanity, surrendering their mirrors for God's house, the figure of laying aside self-love and vanity for God, and the laver itself a figure of cleansing — the washing of the priests, the figure of the washing of regeneration and sanctification, Titus 3:5; Ephesians 5:26, 'the washing of water by the word,' and notably the word of God as a mirror, the laver of brass, once mirrors, the figure of the word that both reflects us to ourselves and cleanses us, James 1:23-25, 'a man beholding his natural face in a glass,' the word the mirror that shows our defilement and the laver that washes it, 2 Corinthians 3:18, beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord); the women who assembled (served, ministered) at the door of the tabernacle (the godly women, devoted to God's service, who gave their mirrors) — the devotion (the women's service and self-denial, giving their vanity to God, the figure, the surrender of self for God's house); the laver of brass — the place of washing (the providence, the laver of cleansing, made of the surrendered mirrors, the figure of self-denial and the cleansing word) — Wesley, Clarke, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What is the significance of the laver made of the women's mirrors?

sides.position: The instruments of vanity surrendered and made into the laver of cleansing (the self-denial of the women, renouncing their vanity for God's house) - the laver a figure of the washing of regeneration and sanctification (Titus 3:5; Ephesians 5:26), the word a mirror that shows and cleanses our defilement (James 1:23-25)

themes: And he made the laver of brass, and the foot of it of brass, of the lookingglasses of the women assembling, which assembled at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation
the laver made of the women's mirrors (the crux of cleansing and self-denial)
the instruments of vanity surrendered for the laver of cleansing, the self-denial of the women
the laver a figure of cleansing, the word a mirror that shows and washes (Titus 3:5; James 1:23-25)

schools.summary: The making of the court with its linen hangings: 'and he made the court: on the south side southward the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, an hundred cubits' — the court (the enclosure around the tabernacle) made with hangings of fine twined linen, a hundred cubits on the south side (Exodus 27:9-19, the court, the outer enclosure, separating the holy space from the common, the white linen hangings, the walls of the court, the figure of purity and righteousness, the dimensions, a hundred cubits, the large court for the people's approach); the fine twined linen (the white hangings, the walls of the court, Exodus 27:9) — the figure (the white linen, the righteousness of the saints, Revelation 19:8, the purity of God's house, the separation of the holy from the common); 'an hundred cubits' (the large court, a hundred cubits, the spacious enclosure for the people, the figure, the court the place of the people's approach, separated by the white walls of righteousness, the providence, the court made, the enclosure of God's house, the figure of the separation and purity) — Henry, Gill, Wesley, Clarke.

themes: And he made the court: on the south side southward the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, an hundred cubits
the making of the court with its linen hangings
the enclosure of God's house, separating the holy from the common (Exodus 27:9-19)
the white walls of righteousness, the purity of God's house (Revelation 19:8)

schools.summary: The sockets, hooks, and fillets of the court pillars: 'and the sockets for the pillars were of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver; and the overlaying of their chapiters of silver; and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver' — the pillars of the court having sockets (bases) of brass, but hooks, fillets (connecting rods or bands), and chapiters (capitals) overlaid with silver (the brass sockets, the bases in the earth, the lower, baser metal, the silver hooks and fillets, the upper ornaments of silver, the redemption metal, the gradation, brass below, silver above, the figure of the ascent from the common to the holy); the brass sockets (the bases of the pillars, Exodus 27:10-11) — the foundation (the pillars set in brass); 'the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver... all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver' (the silver fillets and chapiters, the connecting silver, the atonement-money silver, vv.25-28, the figure, the silver of redemption binding the pillars, the saints joined by the redemption of Christ, the providence, the pillars filleted with silver, the redemption metal binding God's house) — Clarke, Ellicott.

themes: And the sockets for the pillars were of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver; and the overlaying of their chapiters of silver; and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver
the sockets, hooks, and fillets of the court pillars
the gradation, brass below and silver above (Exodus 27:10-11)
the silver of the atonement money binding the pillars, the redemption metal (vv.25-28)

schools.summary: The faithful reckoning of the tabernacle: 'this is the sum of the tabernacle, even of the tabernacle of testimony, as it was counted, according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, son to Aaron the priest' — the account — 'This is the sum of the tabernacle... as it was counted, according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar': a full account (sum, reckoning) made of all the materials of the tabernacle, the gold, silver, and brass, counted by the Levites under Ithamar (Aaron's son, the witnesses noting the significance — the careful accounting of all that was given for God's house, the materials reckoned up, audited, that all might see the offerings rightly used, the lesson — the faithful stewardship of what is given to God, the leaders giving an account, the offerings of the people not embezzled but openly reckoned, providing for honest things in the sight of all, 2 Corinthians 8:20-21, 'avoiding this, that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us; providing for honest things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men,' the transparency, the open account, that the people might know their gifts were faithfully employed, the figure of faithful stewardship in the house of God); the account made by the Levites under Ithamar (the Levites employed in the service, the reckoning by their hand, vv.22-23) — the order (the work and the accounting committed to appointed men, the stewardship, the faithful handling of God's things); the sum of the tabernacle — the full reckoning (the providence, the careful account of God's house, the figure of faithful stewardship and transparency) — Calvin, Wesley, Henry, Gill.

debates.question: What does the careful reckoning of the tabernacle teach?

sides.position: The faithful stewardship of what is given to God - the careful, open accounting of all the offerings, providing for honest things in the sight of all (2 Corinthians 8:20-21), the transparency that the people might know their gifts were rightly employed, a figure of faithful stewardship in the house of God

themes: This is the sum of the tabernacle, even of the tabernacle of testimony, as it was counted, according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar
the faithful reckoning of the tabernacle (the crux of stewardship and accounting)
the careful, open accounting of all given for God's house
faithful stewardship, providing for honest things in the sight of all (2 Corinthians 8:20-21)

schools.summary: Bezaleel making all the LORD commanded: 'and Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the LORD commanded Moses' — Bezaleel (the chief workman, of Judah, Exodus 35:30) making all that the LORD had commanded Moses (the obedience, the work done exactly as commanded, nothing added or omitted, the faithfulness, Bezaleel making all according to the pattern, Exodus 25:40, the chief workman, the master craftsman completing the work); Bezaleel of Judah (the chief workman, the royal tribe) — the gift (the Spirit-filled workman, Exodus 31:2-5, the faithfulness, making all as commanded); 'made all that the LORD commanded Moses' (the work done exactly as commanded, all according to the pattern, nothing of his own invention, the figure, the faithful servant doing all God's will, Hebrews 3:5, the providence, the work of God's house done in exact obedience, the pattern followed) — Calvin, Gill, Keil.

themes: And Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the LORD commanded Moses
Bezaleel making all the LORD commanded
the work done exactly as commanded, according to the pattern (Exodus 25:40)
the faithful servant doing all God's will (Hebrews 3:5)

schools.summary: Aholiab the skilled helper: 'and with him was Aholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, and a cunning workman, and an embroiderer in blue, and in purple, and in scarlet, and fine linen' — with Bezaleel was Aholiab (of Dan), skilled as an engraver, a cunning (artistic) workman, and an embroiderer in the rich colors and fine linen (the helper, Aholiab, the second master workman, of Dan, Exodus 31:6, the skills, engraving, design, embroidery, the two tribes, Judah and Dan, the chief and his helper, from two tribes); Aholiab of Dan (the second workman, the helper of Bezaleel) — the partnership (the two workmen, from two tribes, joined in the work); 'an engraver, and a cunning workman, and an embroiderer in blue, and in purple, and in scarlet, and fine linen' (the various skills, engraving, design, embroidery, in the rich colors, the figure, the variety of gifts joined in the one work, 1 Corinthians 12:4-11, the providence, Aholiab the skilled helper, the gifts joined for God's house) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And with him was Aholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, and a cunning workman, and an embroiderer in blue, and in purple, and in scarlet, and fine linen
Aholiab the skilled helper
the second master workman, of Dan, joined to Bezaleel (Exodus 31:6)
the variety of gifts joined in the one work of God's house (1 Corinthians 12:4-11)

schools.summary: The reckoning of the gold of the offering: 'all the gold that was occupied for the work in all the work of the holy place, even the gold of the offering, was twenty and nine talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary' — the total gold used for the work being twenty-nine talents and seven hundred and thirty shekels (a vast sum, by the sanctuary standard, the gold of the offering, the freely given gold, vv.35:22, the great amount, the abundance of the willing offering, the vast treasure given for God's house); the gold of the offering (the freewill gift, the great sum) — the abundance (the people gave so much that there was more than enough, Exodus 36:5-7, the willing giving, the vast treasure freely offered); 'twenty and nine talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary' (the exact sum, reckoned by the sanctuary shekel, the holy standard, the figure, the abundance of the offering, the precise accounting, the willing gift faithfully reckoned, the providence, the great sum of gold, the abundance of the willing offering for God's house) — Clarke, Gill, Ellicott.

themes: All the gold that was occupied for the work in all the work of the holy place, even the gold of the offering, was twenty and nine talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary
the reckoning of the gold of the offering
the vast treasure of gold freely given, the abundance of the willing offering (Exodus 36:5-7)
the exact sum reckoned by the sanctuary shekel, the holy standard

schools.summary: The silver of the numbered congregation: 'and the silver of them that were numbered of the congregation was an hundred talents, and a thousand seven hundred and threescore and fifteen shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary' — the silver coming from those numbered (counted in the census), the half-shekel each paid (vv.26, the silver, the atonement money, the half-shekel of every man numbered, Exodus 30:11-16, the sum, a hundred talents and more, by the sanctuary shekel, the source, not a freewill offering like the gold, but the appointed half-shekel of every man numbered); the silver from those numbered (the half-shekel of the census, the atonement money, Exodus 30:12-16) — the ransom (the half-shekel a ransom for the soul, the redemption money, the figure, every soul ransomed, the redemption price); 'an hundred talents, and a thousand seven hundred and threescore and fifteen shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary' (the exact sum, the silver reckoned, used for the sockets, vv.27, the providence, the atonement money of the numbered congregation, the redemption silver for God's house) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And the silver of them that were numbered of the congregation was an hundred talents, and a thousand seven hundred and threescore and fifteen shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary
the silver of the numbered congregation
the half-shekel atonement money of every man numbered (Exodus 30:11-16)
the redemption silver, the ransom for the soul, for God's house

schools.summary: The half-shekel ransom for every soul: 'a bekah for every man, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty men' — the atonement money — 'a bekah for every man, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upward': every man numbered (from twenty years old and up) paying a bekah, half a shekel, as the atonement money (the witnesses noting the significance — the half-shekel a ransom for the soul, the atonement money, 'every man a ransom for his soul,' Exodus 30:12-16, 'that there be no plague among them when thou numberest them,' the equal price, rich and poor alike paying the same half-shekel, the soul of all of equal value, the redemption price the same for all, Exodus 30:15, the figure — the ransom of the soul, not with the half-shekel of silver ultimately, but by the precious blood of Christ, 1 Peter 1:18-19, 'ye were not redeemed with corruptible things, as silver and gold... but with the precious blood of Christ,' the redemption of the soul costly, Psalm 49:7-8, the six hundred thousand, the vast number numbered, each ransomed, the great congregation redeemed, the silver of the ransom used for the sockets of the sanctuary, vv.27, the redemption the foundation of God's house); the number numbered (603,550 men, the same as the census, Numbers 1:46) — the vast congregation (each paying the ransom, the figure, the great number redeemed); the half-shekel of every soul — the atonement money, the ransom (the providence, the redemption price of every soul, the figure of the costly ransom by Christ's blood) — Clarke, Keil, Gill.

debates.question: What does the half-shekel atonement money signify?

sides.position: The half-shekel a ransom for the soul, the redemption price of every soul, rich and poor alike (Exodus 30:12-16) - a figure of the ransom of the soul not by silver but by the precious blood of Christ (1 Peter 1:18-19; Psalm 49:7-8), the silver of the ransom the foundation of God's house

themes: A bekah for every man, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty men
the half-shekel ransom for every soul (the crux of the atonement money)
the half-shekel the redemption price of every soul, rich and poor alike (Exodus 30:12-16)
a figure of the ransom not by silver but by the blood of Christ (1 Peter 1:18-19; Psalm 49:7-8)

schools.summary: The sockets cast from the silver of the ransom: 'and of the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the vail; an hundred sockets of the hundred talents, a talent for a socket' — the hundred talents of silver (the atonement money) cast into the hundred sockets (bases) of the sanctuary and the veil, a talent each (the sockets, the silver bases of the boards and the veil-pillars, the foundation of the tabernacle, the silver, the atonement money, the ransom, made the foundation, the figure, the redemption the foundation of God's house, the sanctuary resting on the silver of the ransom); the sockets cast from the atonement silver (the foundation of the tabernacle resting on the ransom money) — the figure (the redemption the foundation, the church built on the ransom of Christ, the sanctuary resting on the atonement, the lesson, the whole structure resting on the silver of the ransom); 'an hundred sockets of the hundred talents, a talent for a socket' (the exact reckoning, a talent each, the hundred sockets, the providence, the atonement silver made the foundation, the figure of redemption underlying God's house) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And of the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the vail; an hundred sockets of the hundred talents, a talent for a socket
the sockets cast from the silver of the ransom
the redemption money made the foundation, the sanctuary resting on the silver of the ransom
the figure of redemption underlying God's house

schools.summary: The silver used for the hooks and chapiters: 'and of the thousand seven hundred seventy and five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their chapiters, and filleted them' — the remaining silver (1,775 shekels, over the hundred talents) used for the hooks of the pillars, the overlaying of the chapiters (capitals), and the fillets (bands, the silver hooks and fillets, the upper ornaments of the court pillars, vv.17, the redemption silver, the atonement money also adorning the pillars, the figure, the redemption silver binding and adorning God's house, above and around); the silver hooks (for the pillars, from the atonement money) — the use (the remaining silver employed for the ornaments); 'overlaid their chapiters, and filleted them' (the chapiters overlaid and the pillars filleted with silver, the atonement silver adorning, vv.17, the figure, the redemption silver binding and adorning the pillars of God's house, the providence, the atonement money used for the foundation and the ornaments, the redemption underlying and adorning God's house) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And of the thousand seven hundred seventy and five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their chapiters, and filleted them
the silver used for the hooks and chapiters
the atonement money used not only for the foundation but for the ornaments (vv.17, 27)
the redemption silver binding and adorning the pillars of God's house

schools.summary: The reckoning of the brass of the offering: 'and the brass of the offering was seventy talents, and two thousand and four hundred shekels' — the total brass freely offered being seventy talents and 2,400 shekels (the brass of the offering, the freewill gift of brass, vv.35:24, the sum, the great amount of brass, by the sanctuary standard, the use, the brass for the altar, the laver, the sockets of the court, the pins, vv.30-31); the brass of the offering (the freewill gift of brass) — the abundance (the brass freely given, the third of the metals reckoned); 'seventy talents, and two thousand and four hundred shekels' (the exact sum, the brass reckoned, the precise account, the providence, the brass of the offering, the freewill gift, used for the court and the altar, vv.30-31, the careful reckoning of all the metals) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And the brass of the offering was seventy talents, and two thousand and four hundred shekels
the reckoning of the brass of the offering
the brass freely given, carefully reckoned (vv.35:24)
used for the court and the altar of God's house (vv.30-31)

schools.summary: The brass made into the sockets, altar, and vessels: 'and therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the brasen altar, and the brasen grate for it, and all the vessels of the altar' — the brass made into the sockets of the door of the tabernacle, the brazen altar, its grate, and all its vessels (the use, the brass for the door-sockets, the altar, and its furniture, the brazen altar, made of the offered brass, the place of sacrifice, the vessels, all the furniture of the altar, of brass); the brass for the door-sockets and the altar (the place of sacrifice, vv.1) — the figure (the brazen altar of sacrifice, the cross); 'the brasen grate for it, and all the vessels of the altar' (the grate and vessels, the furniture of the altar, vv.3-5, the providence, the brass made into the altar and its furniture, the freewill gift employed for the place of sacrifice) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the brasen altar, and the brasen grate for it, and all the vessels of the altar
the brass made into the sockets, altar, and vessels
the freewill brass employed for the door-sockets and the altar of sacrifice (vv.1)
the brazen altar and all its furniture (vv.3-5)

schools.summary: The brass made into the court sockets and pins: 'and the sockets of the court round about, and the sockets of the court gate, and all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins of the court round about' — the brass also made into the sockets (bases) of the court and its gate, and all the pins (tent-pegs) of the tabernacle and the court (the sockets, the brass bases of the court pillars and the gate, the pins, the tent-pegs holding the curtains and hangings firm, the use, the brass for the foundation and the fastenings of the court); the brass sockets of the court and gate (the bases of the pillars, vv.17) — the foundation (the court resting on the brass sockets); 'all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins of the court round about' (the pins, the tent-pegs, the small but necessary fastenings, vv.35:18, the figure, the small things holding God's house firm, every part needful, 1 Corinthians 12:22, the providence, the brass made into the sockets and pins, the foundation and fastenings of God's house, the chapter closing the reckoning of the metals, the whole work supplied by the willing offering) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And the sockets of the court round about, and the sockets of the court gate, and all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins of the court round about
the brass made into the court sockets and pins
the brass for the bases and the tent-pegs, the foundation and fastenings (vv.17; 35:18)
the small things holding God's house firm, every part needful (1 Corinthians 12:22)
